user_query: I have germline exome variants from a patient with a suspected rare Mendelian disorder. I want to identify potentially pathogenic coding variants for clinical interpretation.

most_severe.note: Need full per-transcript detail for clinical review

summary.note: Need detailed annotations, not a summary

justification: Clinical rare-disease interpretation of coding variants needs gene/HGVS/protein identifiers, MANE + canonical transcripts for consistent reporting, missense pathogenicity prediction (SIFT, PolyPhen, CADD), ClinVar clinical significance via co-located known variants, and gnomAD exome+genome frequencies to filter out common variants. Per-transcript detail must be preserved (no most_severe/summary).

user_query: A rare disease patient has a variant near an exon-intron boundary. I want to assess whether it disrupts splicing as part of the diagnostic workup.

most_severe.note: Need per-transcript detail to localise the splice effect

justification: A splice-region variant in a diagnostic context needs a dedicated splice predictor (SpliceAI is the current standard), exon/intron numbering to localise the variant relative to the boundary, HGVS/MANE/canonical for reporting, ClinVar significance, and gnomAD frequencies to assess rarity. (MaxEntScan and dbscSNV are reasonable additional splice predictors — left out here to keep the example to high-confidence choices.)

user_query: I'm analysing somatic variants from tumour whole-exome sequencing of cancer patients, called with Mutect2. What VEP options should I use?

most_severe.note: Keep per-transcript detail for driver interpretation

justification: Somatic variant analysis benefits from flagging known co-located variants (dbSNP/COSMIC via check_existing), ClinVar significance, missense pathogenicity prediction (SIFT/PolyPhen/CADD) to prioritise candidate drivers, and gnomAD exome+genome frequencies to filter out residual germline variants. Per-transcript detail is preserved.

user_query: My GWAS hits are mostly in intergenic and intronic regions. I want to understand their potential regulatory effects.
justification: Non-coding/regulatory variants need regulatory-feature consequences (the Ensembl regulatory build), transcript biotype context, co-located known variants and gnomAD frequency. Missense predictors (SIFT/PolyPhen/CADD) do not apply to non-coding variants. (Plugins such as Enformer/UTRAnnotator could add regulatory-impact signal but are left out as a lower-confidence choice.)

user_query: I want to annotate a large cohort VCF with population allele frequencies to compare variant frequencies across populations.

sift.note: Pathogenicity prediction is not the focus

justification: Population-frequency annotation centres on co-located known variants and multi-source allele frequencies: 1000 Genomes global (af) and continental (af_1kg), plus gnomAD exomes (af_gnomade) and genomes (af_gnomadg). Pathogenicity prediction is secondary for this use case.

user_query: We performed CRISPR knockouts in mouse embryonic stem cells and called variants from WGS against the GRCm39 reference. What VEP settings should I use?
justification: For a non-human species (mouse/GRCm39) the human-only resources — PolyPhen, CADD, ClinVar, gnomAD, REVEL, AlphaMissense — are unavailable and must not be enabled. Use species-agnostic annotation: gene symbol, transcript biotype, HGVS, exon/intron numbers, co-located known variants (mouse has variation data), and SIFT (computed by Ensembl for mouse). This example doubles as the canonical test of the species-restriction safety layer.

user_query: I just want to look up a single variant by its rsID — its gene, consequence, and clinical significance.
justification: A quick single-variant lookup needs only the gene symbol, co-located known-variant lookup (to resolve the rsID), ClinVar clinical significance, and HGVS nomenclature. Heavy predictors and frequency panels are unnecessary overhead for a one-off lookup.
